name: org.machanism.machai:mcp-server-maven-plugin
description: MCP Server Maven Plugin is a Java 17 Maven plugin for launching the Machai MCP Server directly from a Maven build. It exposes Machai AI tools over the Model Context Protocol via HTTP so MCP-compatible clients such as Claude Desktop and other local development, integration testing, demonstration, or build-adjacent AI workflows can connect to a running MCP endpoint without a separate launcher. The plugin bridges Maven project context with the Machai MCP Server runtime: when a goal is invoked it resolves the active Maven project base directory, name, and version; constructs the appropriate server instance; registers available tools through Java SPI discovery using FunctionToolsLoader and GenericGenaiAdapter; and manages the Jetty-backed HTTP server lifecycle for the duration of the Maven goal. It provides two aggregator goals: mcp-server:stateless for independent stateless HTTP requests with no persistent session state using MCP SDK HttpServletStatelessServerTransport, and mcp-server:streamable for incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection using HttpServletStreamableServerTransportProvider. The org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven package contains the abstract and concrete Maven Mojos for configuring and starting stateless and streamable MCP servers, including shared logic for basedir, project metadata, the required mcp.port parameter, environment variables, and credentials. The org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.tools package contains utility function tools for MCP server lifecycle operations such as starting, stopping, and configuring server behavior during Maven build execution.

usageContext: Starting the Machai MCP Server from Maven goals named mcp-server:stateless and mcp-server:streamable
Exposing SPI-discovered Machai AI tools to MCP-compatible clients over HTTP
Running stateless HTTP MCP transport when every request should be handled independently with no persistent session state
Running streamable HTTP MCP transport when clients need incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection
Providing MCP tools with Maven project context including project base directory, project name, and project version
Configuring the HTTP binding port with the required mcp.port Maven parameter or command-line system property
Applying shared environment variable and credential configuration before server startup
Managing a Jetty-backed MCP endpoint for local development, demonstrations, integration tests, and AI-assisted build workflows
Stopping or controlling a Maven-started MCP server through lifecycle function tools

constructors.description: Constructs the abstract base class for MCP server Maven plugin Mojos. The class is intended for extension by concrete Maven goals and centralizes common Maven-injected configuration such as basedir, the active Maven project, the required mcp.port value, and optional parameters used for environment variables and credentials before MCP server startup.

examples.description: Extend the shared abstract Mojo when building a custom Maven goal that needs the same MCP server configuration lifecycle and Maven project context as the built-in goals.

constructors.description: Creates the Maven Mojo for the mcp-server:stateless aggregator goal. Maven instantiates this class when the stateless goal is invoked, injects the active project, base directory, mcp.port configuration, and optional parameters, then the goal starts a stateless HTTP Machai MCP Server that registers available tools and listens until the process is stopped.

examples.description: Install and configure the ready-to-use plugin in pom.xml. Maven constructs org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.HttpStatelessServerMojo automatically when mcp-server:stateless is executed.

constructors.description: Creates the Maven Mojo for the mcp-server:streamable aggregator goal. Maven instantiates this class when the streamable goal is invoked, injects the active Maven project context and configured port, applies shared parameters, and starts a streamable HTTP Machai MCP Server for persistent HTTP transport workflows with incremental result delivery.

examples.description: Install and configure the ready-to-use plugin in pom.xml. Maven constructs org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.HttpStreamableMcpServerMojo automatically when mcp-server:streamable is executed.

constructors.description: Creates a utility function-tools component for managing MCP server lifecycle operations within Maven plugin executions. The package documentation describes this tool as supporting MCP server control operations such as starting, stopping, and configuring server behavior during Maven build execution.

examples.description: Instantiate the lifecycle utility directly in Java tests or embedded experiments that need access to MCP server management functions outside normal Maven goal execution.

features.name: applyParameters
features.description: Applies shared environment and credential configuration before a concrete MCP server goal starts the server. The package documentation identifies shared configuration logic for environment variables and credentials, while the plugin overview explains that both server modes accept the same mcp.port configuration and project-context concepts. Custom Mojos can call this inherited method before constructing or starting MCP-related services.

examples.description: Use the inherited configuration step from a custom Maven Mojo before starting an MCP server so environment variables and credentials are applied consistently with the built-in goals.

features.name: mcp-server:stateless
features.description: Aggregator Maven goal that starts the Machai MCP Server in stateless HTTP mode. The goal constructs an HTTP stateless MCP server, registers SPI-discovered Machai tools, reports identity from the active Maven project name and version, sets the Maven project base directory as the server project directory, binds to the configured mcp.port, and listens until the process is stopped. Stateless mode handles each request independently with no persistent session state.

examples.description: How to use it: run the stateless goal from a Maven project and provide the required port on the command line to expose a local MCP-compatible HTTP endpoint for development or testing.
Invoke the stateless goal with fully qualified Maven plugin coordinates when the plugin prefix is not configured or when an explicit version is required.

features.name: mcp-server:streamable
features.description: Aggregator Maven goal that starts the Machai MCP Server in streamable HTTP mode. The goal constructs an HTTP streamable MCP server, registers the same SPI-discovered Machai tools as stateless mode, reports identity from Maven project name and version, uses the Maven project base directory as project context, binds to the configured mcp.port, and listens until stopped. Streamable mode supports incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection.

examples.description: How to use it: run the streamable goal from a Maven project and provide the required port when client workflows benefit from incremental result delivery over persistent HTTP transport.
Invoke the streamable goal with fully qualified Maven plugin coordinates for reproducible command-line usage.

features.name: MCP server lifecycle utilities
features.description: Provides utility functions for managing the MCP server lifecycle within Maven plugin executions. The package summary describes these tools as supporting MCP server operations such as starting, stopping, and configuring server behavior during Maven build execution. They are useful when an MCP-compatible client or test workflow needs to control a Maven-started server after interactions are complete.

examples.description: Create the lifecycle utility in Java when testing or embedding MCP server management behavior outside the normal Maven plugin goal lifecycle.
How to use it from an MCP-style client: after completing interactions with the Maven-started server, invoke the published shutdown lifecycle tool to request clean termination.

customizations.name: mcp.port
customizations.description: Required Maven plugin configuration parameter that defines the network port used by both the stateless and streamable HTTP MCP server goals. It can be supplied in the plugin configuration block or overridden with -Dmcp.port=... on the command line.

examples.description: Configure the HTTP port in pom.xml so developers can run mcp-server:stateless or mcp-server:streamable without repeating the port value on every command.
Override or provide the required port from the command line for a one-off stateless run.

customizations.name: basedir
customizations.description: Automatically resolved Maven project base directory. The plugin passes this directory to the server runtime as the project directory so SPI-loaded tools can resolve project-relative paths during MCP interactions.

examples.description: Run the goal from the Maven project. The plugin resolves the active project base directory automatically and uses it as MCP server project context.

customizations.name: project name and version
customizations.description: Maven project metadata taken from the active project and reported in the MCP server identity block exposed to connecting clients. This allows clients to identify the server by the current Maven project name and version.

examples.description: The active Maven project name and inherited project version are used by the plugin when identifying the running MCP server to clients.

customizations.name: params
customizations.description: Optional configuration map for environment variables, credentials, and server behavior settings. The package documentation describes shared configuration logic for environment variables and credentials, and the shared base Mojo applies this configuration before concrete stateless or streamable goals start the server.

examples.description: Configure environment values and a Maven settings server identifier so the plugin can apply the values needed by the MCP server and its SPI-loaded tools before startup.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.AbstractMCPServerMojo
studs.description: Abstract base class for MCP server Maven plugin Mojos. It extends the Maven Mojo model for this plugin and provides shared configuration for basedir, project metadata, mcp.port, environment variables, credentials, and other common startup behavior used by the stateless and streamable concrete goal classes.

examples.description: Extend the abstract class when implementing another MCP-oriented Maven Mojo that should share the same configuration and parameter application behavior.

examples.title: Install and run the stateless MCP server goal
examples.scenario: Start a stateless MCP-compatible HTTP endpoint from a Maven project for local development, integration testing, Claude Desktop integration, or demonstrations.
steps: Add org.machanism.machai:mcp-server-maven-plugin:1.2.0 to the build/plugins section of pom.xml or invoke it with fully qualified Maven coordinates.
Configure the required port in the plugin configuration using <port>8080</port>, or pass it from the command line as -Dmcp.port=8080.
Optionally configure params for environment variables, credentials, or server-specific settings that should be applied before startup.
Run mvn mcp-server:stateless -Dmcp.port=8080 from the Maven project directory.
Connect an MCP-compatible client to the HTTP endpoint opened by the plugin-started server.
Use the exposed Machai tools during the development or test session, then stop the Maven process or invoke the lifecycle shutdown tool.
examples.description: Practical installation, configuration, and execution flow for the ready-to-use stateless Maven goal. Stateless mode handles each HTTP request independently and is suitable for simple local MCP endpoint scenarios.

examples.title: Install and run the streamable MCP server goal
examples.scenario: Start a streamable HTTP MCP endpoint when a client workflow benefits from incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection.
steps: Declare the plugin org.machanism.machai:mcp-server-maven-plugin:1.2.0 in pom.xml or call it using fully qualified coordinates.
Set the required HTTP port with <port>8081</port> in the plugin configuration or pass -Dmcp.port=8081 on the command line.
Optionally configure params so environment variables or Maven settings credentials are applied before startup.
Run mvn mcp-server:streamable -Dmcp.port=8081 from the active Maven project.
Connect your MCP-compatible client to the streamable HTTP endpoint and use the same SPI-discovered Machai tools available in stateless mode.
When finished, request clean shutdown through the lifecycle tool or stop the Maven process.
examples.description: Practical installation, configuration, and execution flow for the streamable Maven goal. Streamable mode supports incremental result delivery through streamable HTTP transport while still using Maven project context for server identity and project directory resolution.

examples.title: Apply Maven settings credentials and environment parameters
examples.scenario: Start the MCP server with environment variables and credentials resolved from Maven settings.xml so downstream Machai tools can access required configuration during Maven-hosted server execution.
steps: Add a server entry to Maven settings.xml with the identifier used by the plugin configuration, for example machai-server.
Configure the plugin params map with serverId and any additional environment values needed by the MCP server or SPI-loaded tools.
Run either mcp-server:stateless or mcp-server:streamable with a valid mcp.port value.
The shared Mojo parameter-application step applies configured environment and credential settings before starting the MCP server.
examples.description: Practical configuration flow for using the shared parameter and credential application mechanism documented for org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.AbstractMCPServerMojo.

examples.title: Stop the Maven-started MCP server with the lifecycle tool
examples.scenario: Gracefully terminate the MCP server after completing client interactions.
steps: Start the MCP server with either mvn mcp-server:stateless -Dmcp.port=8080 or mvn mcp-server:streamable -Dmcp.port=8081.
Perform the required interactions from an MCP-compatible client.
Invoke the server lifecycle shutdown tool with an exit code such as 0 for normal termination.
Wait for the Maven-hosted server process to terminate cleanly.
examples.description: Practical how-to scenario for using the lifecycle utility supplied by org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.tools.MCPServerTools to stop a plugin-started server from a client-oriented workflow.
